channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Jimmy Barnes, INXS Lead Tributes to James Valentine Following Death at 64
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to our podcast, where we're taking a moment to remember a legendary figure in Australian music and media. James Valentine, a talented saxophonist and longtime broadcaster, has passed away at the age of 64. Let's take a look at his incredible career and the tributes that are pouring in from across the industry."
[Soft music transitions in]
Host: "James Valentine was best known for his work with the band Models, and later as a broadcaster with the Australian Broadcasting Corporation. He worked with some of Australia's biggest names, including Jimmy Barnes and Iva Davies from Icehouse. In fact, Iva Davies shared a heartfelt statement, remembering James as a 'great musician' who played saxophone on Icehouse's Code Blue album."
[Quick music clip from Icehouse's Code Blue album plays]
Host: "Jimmy Barnes also paid tribute, sharing a touching message on social media. He remembered James as a 'funny and insightful interviewer' and said he'll miss hearing his 'gentle voice' on ABC radio every afternoon."
[Short music clip from Models' Out of Mind, Out of Sight plays]
Host: "James Valentine's music career spanned multiple decades, with his work on Models' Out of Mind, Out of Sight, even peaking at number 84 on the Billboard 200 chart. He also toured and recorded with other notable Australian acts, including INXS, who shared their own tribute to James, remembering him as an 'incredible musician and an amazing human'."
[Outro music starts playing]
Host: "James Valentine's legacy will be deeply felt across the Australian music and media industries. Our thoughts are with his family and loved ones during this difficult time. Thanks for joining us today as we reflect on the life and career of this talented musician and broadcaster."
[Outro music continues to play until the end]
